name: Collados del Asón Park Loop
name_original: Riva, Collado de Asón, Ramales de la Victoria – ruta circular
description: The Collados del Asón Park Loop is a difficult 35.9-mile road cycling route through mountainous terrain with challenging climbs and stunning
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Ruesga traverse a diverse landscape characterized by well-paved and quiet roads. The region features the Ruesga Reservoir, the Asón Hills, and panoramic mountain views, offering varied terrain for cyclists. Roads often lead through forests and provide significant elevation gains, appealing to different fitness levels. This area in Cantabria provides a conducive environment for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Ruesga The most popular road cycling route is Collados del Asón Park Loop, a 35.9 miles (57.7 km) difficult trail that takes 3 hours 26 minutes to complete. This route explores the natural park's varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Summit of Fuente Las Varas – Ampuero Climb loop from Arredondo, a difficult 41.5 miles (66.7 km) path. This route features notable climbs and offers expansive views. Local road cyclists also love the Ramales de la Victoria – Ampuero Climb loop from Ramales de la Victoria, a 31.3 miles (50.4 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 31 minutes. Road cycling around Ruesga is defined by the Ruesga Reservoir, the Asón Hills, and panoramic mountain views. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Ruesga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 4000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Ruesga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Ruesga?
A: Ruesga offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 80 different tours available for exploration.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of road cycling routes in Ruesga?
A: Road cycling routes in Ruesga cater to various fitness levels. You'll find options ranging from easy (9 routes) and moderate (35 routes) to challenging difficult routes (39 routes), ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: Are there routes with significant elevation gain for challenging rides?
A: Yes, Ruesga is known for its varied terrain, including routes with significant elevation gains. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004572183/collados-del-ason-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collados del Asón Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; features over 1,300 meters of elevation gain, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20166806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Alisas – Carlos III Gate loop from Arredondo&lt;/a&gt; climbs over 1,500 meters, appealing to cyclists seeking a challenge.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see along road cycling routes in Ruesga?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. Many routes offer scenic views of the Ruesga Reservoir, traverse the picturesque Asón Hills, and provide panoramic mountain vistas. You can also expect to cycle through refreshing forests, especially on roads leading to areas like La Revilla.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible from road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Asón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent natural feature in the area, particularly beautiful in spring and summer. The landscape of the Asón hills provides picturesque settings for cycling, with the waterfall easily accessible.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by interesting caves or geological formations?
A: While primarily focused on road cycling, some routes may offer proximity to natural caves. Notable caves in the broader area include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839749" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cullalvera Cave&lt;/a&gt; and Cubera Cave, which can be explored off the bike.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Ruesga?
A: The spring and summer months are ideal for road cycling in Ruesga, offering pleasant weather to enjoy the natural beauty, including the Asón Waterfall at its most impressive. Panoramic mountain views are spectacular year-round, becoming even more so with snow-capped peaks in winter.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Ruesga?
A: The road cycling experience in Ruesga is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. Cyclists often praise the well-paved and quiet roads, diverse landscapes, and the variety of routes catering to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Ruesga?
A: Yes, many of the routes in Ruesga are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24575269" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Summit of Fuente Las Varas – Ampuero Climb loop from Arredondo&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31888657" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ramales de la Victoria – Ampuero Climb loop from Ramales de la Victoria&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find scenic viewpoints along the routes?
A: Ruesga offers numerous opportunities for breathtaking views. Cyclists can enjoy panoramic vistas from locations like Fresnedo, extending from Picón Blanco to Sierra del Hornijo. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4347749" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Posadía&lt;/a&gt; is another notable viewpoint. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31904462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Gándara Viewpoint – Ramales de la Victoria loop from Riva&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights a scenic viewpoint.

Q: What are the road conditions like for cycling in Ruesga?
A: Roads in Ruesga are often described as being in 'perfect condition' with minimal vehicle traffic, providing a peaceful and safe environment for road cycling. This allows for enjoyable rides across the varied terrain.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or food along the routes?
A: While specific cafes and pubs directly on every route are not detailed, Ruesga and its surrounding villages offer various establishments where cyclists can stop for refreshments. Exploring the local towns will provide options for food and drink.

Q: Is there parking available for cyclists in Ruesga?
A: Yes, Ruesga and its surrounding areas generally offer parking options in towns and near popular trailheads, making it convenient for cyclists to access the routes. Specific parking details may vary by starting point.
tips: The Collado de Asón is an 11km section with a drop of 530m and an average gradient of 4.81%. The asphalt is perfect, with wooden fences on the curves, horizontal marking on the margins, etc. The traffic is light.

Ramales has well-preserved architecture, with some Indian mansions with beautiful gardens and gates. The Revillagigedo palace (18th century) stands out. However, its most valuable heritage is the group of caves located at the beginning of the century by Alcalde del Río: Covalanas, La Haza, Cullalvera and Sotarriza house magnificent cave paintings inside.
Nice park next to the Asón river. It is the perfect place to relax and cool off in the summer after a long ride on the bike.

faqs.question: Is the Collados del Asón Park Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, the Collados del Asón Park Loop is classified as difficult and is best suited for experienced cyclists with good fitness levels. The route involves significant ascents and descents, including challenging climbs like those found in the Collados del Asón Natural Park.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is predominantly mountainous, featuring limestone outcrops, green rolling hills, and small beech forests. The roads are generally asphalted, though some steeper sections might have mixed surfaces with occasional rough patches or concrete for better grip. You'll experience significant elevation changes as the route traverses river valleys and ascends into the Cantabrian Mountains.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights or points of interest along the Collados del Asón Park Loop?
faqs.answer: This loop offers stunning natural beauty and notable locations. You'll pass through the scenic Collado de Asón, the charming settlement of Ramales de la Victoria, and the picturesque Esquileña Park and Asón River. The route provides expansive views of the Cantabrian mountain range and lush meadows.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride the Collados del Asón Park Loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this loop is typically during spring and autumn. These seasons offer milder temperatures, making the demanding climbs more manageable. Summer can be hot, and winter often brings snow to the higher elevations, making cycling more challenging or impossible.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations or permits required for cycling in the Parque Natural de los Collados del Asón?
faqs.answer: Generally, no specific permits are required for road cycling on public roads within the Parque Natural de los Collados del Asón. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked routes, and be mindful of the natural environment. Traffic is typically light, especially on many climbs, contributing to a tranquil cycling environment.

faqs.question: Is the Collados del Asón Park Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling on public roads, so dogs are generally allowed if they are safely secured (e.g., in a bike trailer or basket) and do not interfere with traffic or other road users. However, it's not a traditional hiking trail where dogs can run freely. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you adhere to any local leash laws or regulations if you stop in towns or natural areas.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Collados del Asón Park Loop?
faqs.answer: While the Komoot tour starts near Riva, you can typically find parking in the larger settlements along the route, such as Ramales de la Victoria. Look for public parking areas within these towns, which often serve as convenient starting points for circular routes.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling or hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Collados del Asón Park Loop intersects with or runs alongside several other paths for short sections. These include parts of the [CIMA CA08] Asón - La Sía, Camino del Val del Asón (Nacimiento del río Asón), and the Ruta de Carlos V. You may also encounter sections of the GR-74.

faqs.question: What makes the climbs on this route particularly challenging?
faqs.answer: The climbs on this route are challenging due to their significant length and steep gradients. While the overall average gradient might seem moderate, sections can be very demanding. For instance, the Collado de Asón from Regules is a 13 km climb with 514 meters of vertical ascent. The region is also home to infamous climbs like Los Machucos (Collado Espina), known for ramps between 15% and 20%, and even reaching 25-28% in places, though this specific loop may not include its most extreme sections.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or find accommodation near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in the larger towns and villages along or near the route. Ramales de la Victoria is a good example of a settlement where you can find services. It's advisable to check opening hours, especially in smaller villages, and consider booking accommodation in advance during peak seasons.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while cycling?
faqs.answer: The Collados del Asón Natural Park is known for its diverse ecosystems. While cycling, you might spot various bird species, and if you're lucky, some local mammals. The park's tranquil setting and lush landscapes provide a habitat for a range of flora and fauna, offering a true immersion in nature.
